unitTitle: KS1 Y2 IT around us (Teach Computing — adapted)

lessons.title: What is IT?
lessons.objectives:
I can name some computers
I can name the parts of a computer
I can say what a computer is used for
I can sort things into IT and not IT
lessons.outline:
Routine first (same every lesson). Vocabulary on board: computer, IT (information technology). TA: everyone has the starter and activity worksheet; have a few real items ready (a tablet, a pencil). Read the four "I can…" aloud.
1. (0–5) Starter — what is IT? On the starter worksheet pupils CHOOSE the meaning of IT (a computer, or something that works with a computer), then tick yes/no on a few items.  • Support: yes/no — is a laptop/banana a computer?  • Core: name one computer used at school.  • Challenge: multi-select the computers.
2. (5–15) I-do → we-do — IT means information technology: a computer, or something that works with a computer. Show the desktop computer image and name the parts. Likely error: calling the whole thing "the screen" → fix-words "the screen is just the part we look at."
3. (15–30) Name the parts — pupils do the LABEL task (screen, keyboard, mouse, computer) on the activity worksheet. Then multi-select what a computer can help us do. TA: prompt, do not drag it for them.
4. (30–45) IT or not IT? Pupils SORT items into IT / not IT (card-sort). Scaffolding prompts: "Is it a computer? Does it work with a computer?" Then a calm IT hunt; they photograph one piece for show-your-work.
5. (45–50) Plenary — self-tick the four "I can…"; each pupil names one piece of IT and why it is IT.
Differentiation on the SAME task, low floor / high ceiling — nobody's sheet looks "less done". No flashing/sound; movement breaks are routine.

lessons.title: IT in school
lessons.objectives:
I can name IT in my school
I can sort school IT by what it is used for
I can say that some IT can be used in more than one way
lessons.outline:
Routine first. Vocabulary on board: IT (information technology). TA: everyone has the starter and activity worksheet; point out IT in the room. Read the three "I can…" aloud.
1. (0–5) Starter — is it IT? Thumbs up/down for pictures; on the starter worksheet pupils choose yes/no.  • Support: yes/no — is a tablet/printer/teddy IT?  • Core: name one piece of IT in the classroom.  • Challenge: multi-select the school IT.
2. (5–15) I-do → we-do — look around the room and list the IT we can see (tablets, whiteboard, printer). Likely error: "a teddy is IT" → fix-words "it does not work with a computer."
3. (15–35) Sorting IT — pupils SORT school IT into what it is used for (write or make / find things out / play and learn) on the activity worksheet (card-sort). Scaffolding: "What job would you do with it?" TA: prompt, do not sort it for them.
4. (35–45) Some IT does more than one job — Challenge multi-select: all the jobs a tablet can do. Then show-your-work: photograph a piece of classroom IT.
5. (45–50) Plenary — self-tick the three "I can…"; each pupil names a device and its job.
Genuine S/C/C on the SAME task. No flashing/sound; movement breaks are routine.

lessons.title: IT in the world
lessons.objectives:
I can find examples of IT
I can sort IT by where it is found
I can talk about what IT is used for
lessons.outline:
Routine first. Vocabulary on board: IT (information technology), computer. TA: everyone has the starter and activity worksheet. Read the three "I can…" aloud.
1. (0–5) Starter — where do we find IT? Thumbs up/down for places (shop, woods, street, sea); on the worksheet pupils choose.  • Support: yes/no — is there IT in a shop/street/sea?  • Core: name a place outside school with IT.  • Challenge: multi-select the places.
2. (5–15) I-do → we-do — IT is all around us: shops, the street, lots of jobs. Show the card-reader / cash-machine images. Likely error: "IT is only in school" → fix-words "a shop till is IT too."
3. (15–35) Where is the IT found? Pupils SORT IT into in a shop / on the street (card-sort) on the activity worksheet. Then a TALK-ABOUT: pick one piece and say where it is and what it does (Core sentence "It is found… It helps us…"). TA: prompt, do not answer for them.
4. (35–45) Challenge — the crossing button: multi-select how it helps. Show-your-work: think of a grown-up's job and one piece of IT they use; add a picture.
5. (45–50) Plenary — IT is nearly everywhere; can you think of a job with NO IT? Self-tick the three "I can…".
Genuine S/C/C on the SAME task. No flashing/sound; movement breaks are routine.

lessons.title: The benefits of IT
lessons.objectives:
I can recognise common types of IT
I can show how IT devices work together
I can say why we use IT
lessons.outline:
Routine first. Vocabulary on board: IT, computer, barcode, scanner/scan. TA: everyone has the starter and activity worksheet; set up the till role-play (product list, price list). Read the three "I can…" aloud.
1. (0–5) Starter — IT in a supermarket. Show the barcode image; on the worksheet pupils choose what a scanner reads (the price).  • Support: yes/no — is a till/scanner/tractor in a supermarket?  • Core: what does a scanner do?  • Challenge: multi-select IT at a till.
2. (5–15) I-do → we-do — a barcode is a number for one product; the scanner reads it; the computer looks up the price; the screen shows it. Pupils ORDER the till steps on the activity worksheet.
3. (15–40) You-do — the till role-play (shopper / scanner / price finder). Swap roles so everyone tries each part. Focus on the PROCESS, not the maths. Likely error: doing all jobs at once → fix-words "each part does just one job." TA: keep the steps visible; prompt the next step.
4. (40–47) Why do we use IT? Challenge multi-select (saves time, quick, helps us not forget). Show-your-work: photograph the receipt / the group working.
5. (47–50) Plenary — self-tick the three "I can…"; one reason we use IT.
Genuine S/C/C on the SAME task. No flashing/sound; movement breaks are routine.

lessons.title: Using IT safely
lessons.objectives:
I can list different uses of IT
I can talk about rules for using IT
I can say how rules keep me safe
lessons.outline:
Routine first. Vocabulary on board: IT (information technology), rule, safe. TA: everyone has the starter and activity worksheet. Read the three "I can…" aloud. Note: this touches online safety — follow the school policy if anything is disclosed.
1. (0–5) Starter — what can we do with IT? Multi-select with a tablet; list devices pupils use at home.  • Support: yes/no — can you paint/watch a video/bake a real cake on it?  • Core: name a home device and one use.  • Challenge: multi-select which are IT.
2. (5–15) I-do → we-do — there are RULES for using IT; rules keep us safe and look after the device. Read each rule aloud. Likely error: thinking sharing a password is kind → fix-words "a password is a secret."
3. (15–35) Safe or not safe? Pupils SORT choices into a safe choice / not a safe choice (card-sort), then MATCH each rule to why it helps (Core). TA: prompt, do not match it for them.
4. (35–45) Challenge — pick a rule and say how it keeps you safe ("This rule keeps me safe because…"). Show-your-work: write one rule they will follow; photograph it.
5. (45–50) Plenary — safe AND kind with photos (ask first, check the background). Self-tick the three "I can…".
Genuine S/C/C on the SAME task. No flashing/sound; movement breaks are routine.

lessons.title: Using IT in different ways
lessons.objectives:
I can see the choices I make when using IT
I can use IT for different types of activities
I can explain why we use IT in different ways
lessons.outline:
Routine first. Vocabulary on board: IT (information technology), choice. TA: everyone has the starter and activity worksheet; set up activity stations (connect, get creative, be active, be mindful). Read the three "I can…" aloud.
1. (0–5) Starter — your favourite IT activity (text). Big idea: try a MIX, not just one thing.  • Support: yes/no — can you video call / paint / make IT sleep for you?  • Core: multi-select things you can do with IT.  • Challenge: why use IT in lots of ways?
2. (5–15) I-do → we-do — different TYPES of IT activity: connect, get creative, be active or rest. Model one example for each. Pupils SORT activities into the types on the activity worksheet (card-sort), then MATCH activity → type (Core).
3. (15–40) You-do — the stations; pupils try each type. Allow transition time between activities. Likely error: wanting to stay on a favourite → fix-words "we are trying a mix today." TA: prompt the swap; keep it calm. They photograph what they made/did for show-your-work.
4. (40–47) Why use IT in different ways? Challenge written answer. Plenary: how does IT help us; what do you like/dislike?
5. (47–50) Self-tick the three "I can…"; name two different ways to use IT.
Genuine S/C/C on the SAME task. No flashing/sound; movement breaks are routine.
